'language': 'en'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shimada Shrine}} [[JA:嶋田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876134}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Shimada Shrine | image = [[画像:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai 2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} Yashima-cho 325 | deity = Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto; Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) | status = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}; former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; koshin-heihaku offering shrine | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | cultural_property = Honden (Nara City designated cultural property) }} '''Shimada Shrine''' (しまだじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Yashima-cho, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (small shrine), formerly ranked as a village shrine. === History === The deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} is a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. The shrine name is connected with traditions about descendants of this line and the "Shimada" surname.''Shinsen Shojiroku''. From the late Heian period onward, ties with {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} deepened. Together with other shikinaisha, it was invited to Kasuga's sacred mountain area and worshiped as one of four deities at Kasuga's Kii Shrine. Later records place the shrine at several locations around Yashima. In 1886, when the area of present Yashima Mausoleum was developed as Prince Sawara's mausoleum, the shrine and Sudo Tenno Shrine were moved and merged at the current site. === Enshrined deities === * Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto * Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) === Precinct shrines === * Amaterasu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} * Kasuga Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and Wa-no-shita Okami * Small shrine enshrining Konjin === Main sanctuary === The honden is an old-style Kasuga-zukuri building (2.53 m x 1.92 m) with decorative metal fittings and a large porch. It is regarded as a transferred former Kasuga building ("Kasuga-utsushi"), later moved again at the time of merger in 1886. Different theories exist on the precise transfer date and original hall. It was designated a Nara City cultural property on March 1, 1982. === Stone lanterns === Because of the merger with Sudo Tenno Shrine, many lanterns bear inscriptions such as "Sudo Tenno" and "Yashima Tenno," including examples dated 1640, 1710, and 1754. === Gallery === File:Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai.jpg|Honden viewed from precinct File:Shimada Shrine Nara Honden.jpg|Honden close-up File:Shimada Shrine Nara Shinsen.jpg|Shinsenjo File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu A.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu N.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Amaterasu.jpg|Amaterasu precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara AmenoKoyane.jpg|Kasuga precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara KinShin.jpg|Konjin precinct shrine === Notes === {{Reflist|group="脚注"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * ''Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines'' * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 29: Nara Prefecture'' * ''Yamato-Kii Jiin Jinja Daijiten'' * ''Nara Basin Historical Geography Database'' * ''Nara City History: Shrines and Temples'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sudo Tenno Shrine|Q24876234|WD=Q24876234|1=Sudo Tenno Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Goryo faith|Q3273210|WD=Q3273210|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga-utsushi|Q24865198|WD=Q24865198|1=Kasuga utsushi}} === External links === {{commonscat|Shimada Shrine}} {{osm box|w|418061743}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1336694783322/index.html Shimada Shrine Honden - Nara City] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:奈良県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

'language': 'en'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shimada Shrine}} [[JA:嶋田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876134}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Shimada Shrine | image = [[画像:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai 2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} Yashima-cho 325 | deity = Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto; Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) | status = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}; former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; koshin-heihaku offering shrine | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | cultural_property = Honden (Nara City designated cultural property) }} '''Shimada Shrine''' (しまだじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Yashima-cho, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (small shrine), formerly ranked as a village shrine. === History === The deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} is a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. The shrine name is connected with traditions about descendants of this line and the "Shimada" surname.''Shinsen Shojiroku''. From the late Heian period onward, ties with {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} deepened. Together with other shikinaisha, it was invited to Kasuga's sacred mountain area and worshiped as one of four deities at Kasuga's Kii Shrine. Later records place the shrine at several locations around Yashima. In 1886, when the area of present Yashima Mausoleum was developed as Prince Sawara's mausoleum, the shrine and Sudo Tenno Shrine were moved and merged at the current site. === Enshrined deities === * Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto * Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) === Precinct shrines === * Amaterasu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} * Kasuga Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and Wa-no-shita Okami * Small shrine enshrining Konjin === Main sanctuary === The honden is an old-style Kasuga-zukuri building (2.53 m x 1.92 m) with decorative metal fittings and a large porch. It is regarded as a transferred former Kasuga building ("Kasuga-utsushi"), later moved again at the time of merger in 1886. Different theories exist on the precise transfer date and original hall. It was designated a Nara City cultural property on March 1, 1982. === Stone lanterns === Because of the merger with Sudo Tenno Shrine, many lanterns bear inscriptions such as "Sudo Tenno" and "Yashima Tenno," including examples dated 1640, 1710, and 1754. === Gallery === File:Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai.jpg|Honden viewed from precinct File:Shimada Shrine Nara Honden.jpg|Honden close-up File:Shimada Shrine Nara Shinsen.jpg|Shinsenjo File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu A.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu N.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Amaterasu.jpg|Amaterasu precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara AmenoKoyane.jpg|Kasuga precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara KinShin.jpg|Konjin precinct shrine === Notes === {{Reflist|group="脚注"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * ''Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines'' * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 29: Nara Prefecture'' * ''Yamato-Kii Jiin Jinja Daijiten'' * ''Nara Basin Historical Geography Database'' * ''Nara City History: Shrines and Temples'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sudo Tenno Shrine|Q24876234|WD=Q24876234|1=Sudo Tenno Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Goryo faith|Q3273210|WD=Q3273210|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga-utsushi|Q24865198|WD=Q24865198|1=Kasuga utsushi}} === External links === {{commonscat|Shimada Shrine}} {{osm box|w|418061743}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1336694783322/index.html Shimada Shrine Honden - Nara City] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:奈良県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

Later records place the shrine at several locations around Yashima. In 1886, when the area of present Yashima Mausoleum was developed as Prince Sawara's mausoleum, the shrine and Sudo Tenno Shrine were moved and merged at the current site. === Enshrined deities === * Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto * Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) === Precinct shrines === * Amaterasu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} * Kasuga Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and Wa-no-shita Okami * Small shrine enshrining Konjin === Main sanctuary === The honden is an old-style Kasuga-zukuri building (2.53 m x 1.92 m) with decorative metal fittings and a large porch. It is regarded as a transferred former Kasuga building ("Kasuga-utsushi"), later moved again at the time of merger in 1886. Different theories exist on the precise transfer date and original hall. It was designated a Nara City cultural property on March 1, 1982. === Stone lanterns === Because of the merger with Sudo Tenno Shrine, many lanterns bear inscriptions such as "Sudo Tenno" and "Yashima Tenno," including examples dated 1640, 1710, and 1754. === Gallery === File:Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai.jpg|Honden viewed from precinct File:Shimada Shrine Nara Honden.jpg|Honden close-up File:Shimada Shrine Nara Shinsen.jpg|Shinsenjo File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu A.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu N.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Amaterasu.jpg|Amaterasu precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara AmenoKoyane.jpg|Kasuga precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara KinShin.jpg|Konjin precinct shrine === Notes === {{Reflist|group="脚注"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * ''Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines'' * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 29: Nara Prefecture'' * ''Yamato-Kii Jiin Jinja Daijiten'' * ''Nara Basin Historical Geography Database'' * ''Nara City History: Shrines and Temples'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sudo Tenno Shrine|Q24876234|WD=Q24876234|1=Sudo Tenno Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Goryo faith|Q3273210|WD=Q3273210|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga-utsushi|Q24865198|WD=Q24865198|1=Kasuga utsushi}} === External links === {{commonscat|Shimada Shrine}} {{osm box|w|418061743}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1336694783322/index.html Shimada Shrine Honden - Nara City] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:奈良県内の市町村指定有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

'language': 'en'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shimada Shrine}} [[JA:嶋田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876134}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Shimada Shrine | image = [[画像:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai 2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} Yashima-cho 325 | deity = Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto; Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) | status = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}; former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; koshin-heihaku offering shrine | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | cultural_property = Honden (Nara City designated cultural property) }} '''Shimada Shrine''' (しまだじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Yashima-cho, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (small shrine), formerly ranked as a village shrine. === History === The deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} is a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. The shrine name is connected with traditions about descendants of this line and the "Shimada" surname.''Shinsen Shojiroku''. From the late Heian period onward, ties with {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} deepened. Together with other shikinaisha, it was invited to Kasuga's sacred mountain area and worshiped as one of four deities at Kasuga's Kii Shrine. Later records place the shrine at several locations around Yashima. In 1886, when the area of present Yashima Mausoleum was developed as Prince Sawara's mausoleum, the shrine and Sudo Tenno Shrine were moved and merged at the current site. === Enshrined deities === * Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto * Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) === Precinct shrines === * Amaterasu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} * Kasuga Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and Wa-no-shita Okami * Small shrine enshrining Konjin === Main sanctuary === The honden is an old-style Kasuga-zukuri building (2.53 m x 1.92 m) with decorative metal fittings and a large porch. It is regarded as a transferred former Kasuga building ("Kasuga-utsushi"), later moved again at the time of merger in 1886. Different theories exist on the precise transfer date and original hall. It was designated a Nara City cultural property on March 1, 1982. === Stone lanterns === Because of the merger with Sudo Tenno Shrine, many lanterns bear inscriptions such as "Sudo Tenno" and "Yashima Tenno," including examples dated 1640, 1710, and 1754. === Gallery === File:Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai.jpg|Honden viewed from precinct File:Shimada Shrine Nara Honden.jpg|Honden close-up File:Shimada Shrine Nara Shinsen.jpg|Shinsenjo File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu A.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu N.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Amaterasu.jpg|Amaterasu precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara AmenoKoyane.jpg|Kasuga precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara KinShin.jpg|Konjin precinct shrine === Notes === {{Reflist|group="脚注"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * ''Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines'' * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 29: Nara Prefecture'' * ''Yamato-Kii Jiin Jinja Daijiten'' * ''Nara Basin Historical Geography Database'' * ''Nara City History: Shrines and Temples'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sudo Tenno Shrine|Q24876234|WD=Q24876234|1=Sudo Tenno Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Goryo faith|Q3273210|WD=Q3273210|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga-utsushi|Q24865198|WD=Q24865198|1=Kasuga utsushi}} === External links === {{commonscat|Shimada Shrine}} {{osm box|w|418061743}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1336694783322/index.html Shimada Shrine Honden - Nara City] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

'language': 'en'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shimada Shrine}} [[JA:嶋田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876134}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Shimada Shrine | image = [[画像:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai 2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} Yashima-cho 325 | deity = Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto; Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) | status = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}; former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; koshin-heihaku offering shrine | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | cultural_property = Honden (Nara City designated cultural property) }} '''Shimada Shrine''' (しまだじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Yashima-cho, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (small shrine), formerly ranked as a village shrine. === History === The deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} is a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. The shrine name is connected with traditions about descendants of this line and the "Shimada" surname.''Shinsen Shojiroku''. From the late Heian period onward, ties with {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} deepened. Together with other shikinaisha, it was invited to Kasuga's sacred mountain area and worshiped as one of four deities at Kasuga's Kii Shrine. Later records place the shrine at several locations around Yashima. In 1886, when the area of present Yashima Mausoleum was developed as Prince Sawara's mausoleum, the shrine and Sudo Tenno Shrine were moved and merged at the current site. === Enshrined deities === * Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto * Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) === Precinct shrines === * Amaterasu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} * Kasuga Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and Wa-no-shita Okami * Small shrine enshrining Konjin === Main sanctuary === The honden is an old-style Kasuga-zukuri building (2.53 m x 1.92 m) with decorative metal fittings and a large porch. It is regarded as a transferred former Kasuga building ("Kasuga-utsushi"), later moved again at the time of merger in 1886. Different theories exist on the precise transfer date and original hall. It was designated a Nara City cultural property on March 1, 1982. === Stone lanterns === Because of the merger with Sudo Tenno Shrine, many lanterns bear inscriptions such as "Sudo Tenno" and "Yashima Tenno," including examples dated 1640, 1710, and 1754. === Gallery === File:Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai.jpg|Honden viewed from precinct File:Shimada Shrine Nara Honden.jpg|Honden close-up File:Shimada Shrine Nara Shinsen.jpg|Shinsenjo File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu A.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu N.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Amaterasu.jpg|Amaterasu precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara AmenoKoyane.jpg|Kasuga precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara KinShin.jpg|Konjin precinct shrine === Notes === {{Reflist|group="脚注"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * ''Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines'' * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 29: Nara Prefecture'' * ''Yamato-Kii Jiin Jinja Daijiten'' * ''Nara Basin Historical Geography Database'' * ''Nara City History: Shrines and Temples'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sudo Tenno Shrine|Q24876234|WD=Q24876234|1=Sudo Tenno Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Goryo faith|Q3273210|WD=Q3273210|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga-utsushi|Q24865198|WD=Q24865198|1=Kasuga utsushi}} === External links === {{commonscat|Shimada Shrine}} {{osm box|w|418061743}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1336694783322/index.html Shimada Shrine Honden - Nara City] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]][[Category:qq]]

'language': 'en'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg {{commons category|Category:Shimada Shrine}} [[JA:嶋田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q24876134}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Shimada Shrine | image = [[画像:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai 2.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} Yashima-cho 325 | deity = Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto; Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) | status = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small)|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}; former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; koshin-heihaku offering shrine | architecture = One-bay {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | cultural_property = Honden (Nara City designated cultural property) }} '''Shimada Shrine''' (しまだじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Yashima-cho, {{ill|lt=Nara|Q169134|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha (small shrine), formerly ranked as a village shrine. === History === The deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} is a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. The shrine name is connected with traditions about descendants of this line and the "Shimada" surname.''Shinsen Shojiroku''. From the late Heian period onward, ties with {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} deepened. Together with other shikinaisha, it was invited to Kasuga's sacred mountain area and worshiped as one of four deities at Kasuga's Kii Shrine. Later records place the shrine at several locations around Yashima. In 1886, when the area of present Yashima Mausoleum was developed as Prince Sawara's mausoleum, the shrine and Sudo Tenno Shrine were moved and merged at the current site. === Enshrined deities === * Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto * Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) === Precinct shrines === * Amaterasu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} * Kasuga Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and Wa-no-shita Okami * Small shrine enshrining Konjin === Main sanctuary === The honden is an old-style Kasuga-zukuri building (2.53 m x 1.92 m) with decorative metal fittings and a large porch. It is regarded as a transferred former Kasuga building ("Kasuga-utsushi"), later moved again at the time of merger in 1886. Different theories exist on the precise transfer date and original hall. It was designated a Nara City cultural property on March 1, 1982. === Stone lanterns === Because of the merger with Sudo Tenno Shrine, many lanterns bear inscriptions such as "Sudo Tenno" and "Yashima Tenno," including examples dated 1640, 1710, and 1754. === Gallery === File:Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai.jpg|Honden viewed from precinct File:Shimada Shrine Nara Honden.jpg|Honden close-up File:Shimada Shrine Nara Shinsen.jpg|Shinsenjo File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu A.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu N.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Amaterasu.jpg|Amaterasu precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara AmenoKoyane.jpg|Kasuga precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara KinShin.jpg|Konjin precinct shrine === Notes === {{Reflist|group="脚注"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * ''Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines'' * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 29: Nara Prefecture'' * ''Yamato-Kii Jiin Jinja Daijiten'' * ''Nara Basin Historical Geography Database'' * ''Nara City History: Shrines and Temples'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sudo Tenno Shrine|Q24876234|WD=Q24876234|1=Sudo Tenno Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Goryo faith|Q3273210|WD=Q3273210|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga-utsushi|Q24865198|WD=Q24865198|1=Kasuga utsushi}} === External links === {{commonscat|Shimada Shrine}} {{osm box|w|418061743}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1336694783322/index.html Shimada Shrine Honden - Nara City] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]]

It is a shikinaisha (small shrine), formerly ranked as a village shrine. === History === The deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} is a son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. The shrine name is connected with traditions about descendants of this line and the "Shimada" surname.''Shinsen Shojiroku''. From the late Heian period onward, ties with {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} deepened. Together with other shikinaisha, it was invited to Kasuga's sacred mountain area and worshiped as one of four deities at Kasuga's Kii Shrine. In 1886, when the area of present Yashima Mausoleum was developed as Prince Sawara's mausoleum, the shrine and Sudo Tenno Shrine were moved and merged at the current site. === Enshrined deities === * Kamuyaimimi-no-Mikoto * Sudo Tenno ({{ill|lt=Prince Sawara|Q1079634|WD=Q1079634|1=Prince Sawara}}) === Precinct shrines === * Amaterasu Shrine - {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}} * Kasuga Shrine - {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and Wa-no-shita Okami * Small shrine enshrining Konjin === Main sanctuary === The honden is an old-style Kasuga-zukuri building (2.53 m x 1.92 m) with decorative metal fittings and a large porch. It is regarded as a transferred former Kasuga building ("Kasuga-utsushi"), later moved again at the time of merger in 1886. Different theories exist on the precise transfer date and original hall. It was designated a Nara City cultural property on March 1, 1982. === Stone lanterns === Because of the merger with Sudo Tenno Shrine, many lanterns bear inscriptions such as "Sudo Tenno" and "Yashima Tenno," including examples dated 1640, 1710, and 1754. === Gallery === File:Shimada Shrine Nara Haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Shimada Shrine Nara Keidai.jpg|Honden viewed from precinct File:Shimada Shrine Nara Honden.jpg|Honden close-up File:Shimada Shrine Nara Shinsen.jpg|Shinsenjo File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu A.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Komainu N.jpg|Komainu File:Shimada Shrine Nara Amaterasu.jpg|Amaterasu precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara AmenoKoyane.jpg|Kasuga precinct shrine File:Shimada Shrine Nara KinShin.jpg|Konjin precinct shrine === Notes === {{Reflist|group="脚注"}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * ''Nara Prefectural History 5: Shrines'' * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 29: Nara Prefecture'' * ''Yamato-Kii Jiin Jinja Daijiten'' * ''Nara Basin Historical Geography Database'' * ''Nara City History: Shrines and Temples'' === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sudo Tenno Shrine|Q24876234|WD=Q24876234|1=Sudo Tenno Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Goryo faith|Q3273210|WD=Q3273210|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga-utsushi|Q24865198|WD=Q24865198|1=Kasuga utsushi}} === External links === {{commonscat|Shimada Shrine}} {{osm box|w|418061743}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1336694783322/index.html Shimada Shrine Honden - Nara City] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shimada Shrine]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nara Prefecture]]
